title: agentwork/v2 frontmatter (AIP-20)
description: Validates the YAML frontmatter portion of an AIP-20 WORK.md (workspace root or per-context view). The single doctype 'work.workspace/v2' is used in both modes; the host distinguishes by checking whether `extends` is set. Per-item-kind schemas are delegated to AIP-18 (COLLECTION.md / ITEM.md).

schema.description: Discriminator for the AIP-20 workspace doctype.

name.description: Stable kebab-case identifier for the workspace or view.

description.description: One-paragraph statement of purpose: what this workspace tracks and who uses it.

version.description: Semantic version of the WORKSPACE shape. Bump on collection / scope / rollup / lint / defaults changes. Independent of the tracker's content version.

extends.description: OPTIONAL — relative path to a parent WORK.md. Presence makes the manifest a VIEW; absence makes it a WORKSPACE ROOT. Recursive composition; maximum chain depth is 8.

items.description: Either a ws:// ref to an AIP-9 operator / AIP-6 company / AIP-3 skill, or a relative path to a consumer workspace folder.

appliesTo.description: OPTIONAL — list of consumers this VIEW adapts the workspace for. Hosts MUST refuse the view if any binding does not resolve. Not inherited; views declare their own scope.

executor.description: OPTIONAL — AIP-9 default executor operator. The host activates this operator when an item with no explicit assignee surfaces.

governance.description: OPTIONAL — AIP-7 policy or audit binding. May be a path to an AIP-7 policy file or a ws:// ref. Status-transition approvals, owner-change audits, and scope-widening interventions flow through this ref.

knowledge.description: OPTIONAL — AIP-10 KNOWLEDGE.md ref. Lets cross-references on items resolve against the bound wiki by default.

agency.description: OPTIONAL — AIP-8 agency context. Binds the workspace to an autonomous-agency engagement for billable work, time-tracking, and contractual approval.

playbook.description: OPTIONAL — AIP-12 active playbook. Governs the routine plays this workspace runs.

collections.description: Collections enabled by this workspace. Three forms supported: inline (full COLLECTION.md frontmatter), file ref, or registry import. Merge-by-effective-name (alias if set, otherwise the collection's name) across the extends chain.

enabled.description: Whether the containment axis is active. ONE-WAY SWITCH: once true at any ancestor, descendants MUST NOT set false (would orphan items). Refusal: work_scope_disable (HARD).

field.description: Item field name carrying the containment ref. Defaults to AIP-18's universal-ish 'parent'.

allowedKinds.description: OPTIONAL — collection names allowed as containment parents. The host enforces this at item-write time.

maxDepth.description: OPTIONAL — maximum containment depth. The host counts ancestors and refuses items past the cap.

containment.description: Containment axis (parent/child). Per-collection schemas may declare their own field name; this declaration is the workspace-level default.

enabled.description: Whether the applicability axis is active.

field.description: Item field name carrying the applicability ref list.

valueClass.description: Class of refs the field accepts. Recognised classes: company, role, role-and-company, operator. ONE-WAY SWITCH: once set at any ancestor, descendants MUST NOT change (would invalidate existing items). Refusal: work_scope_value_class_drift (HARD).

applicability.description: Applicability axis (who the item is about / scoped to).

enabled.description: Whether the ownership axis is active.

field.description: Workspace-level default item field name carrying the ownership ref. Per-collection AIP-18 schemas may override this with their own ownership.role.

policy.description: Workspace-level ownership policy. 'strict' requires every collection's ownership.required to be true; 'inherit' delegates to the collection's own setting; 'open' permits any collection to omit ownership.

ownership.description: Ownership axis (who is doing the item). Composes with AIP-18 per-collection ownership rules.

scope.description: Workspace-level scope axes — three orthogonal axes (containment / applicability / ownership) declared once per workspace, applied uniformly across enabled collections.

enabled.description: Whether status rollup is active. When false, parent-item statuses are read directly from the item's stored status.

when.description: Rollup predicate. Recognised values: all-children-terminal, any-child-blocked, any-child-overdue, no-children, custom:<id>. Merge key when composing.

bubbleParentStatus.description: Status id to surface on the parent when the predicate holds. MUST exist in every collection eligible to be a parent (per scope.containment.rules.allowedKinds).

policy.description: Rollup rules. Merge-by-`when` against the extends parent.

exposeViaField.description: OPTIONAL — item field name on parents to materialize the rolled status into. When unset, rollup is query-time only and never written to disk.

statusRollup.description: Status-rollup policy. Per-collection statuses live on AIP-18; this declaration is the workspace-level aggregation layer.

id.description: Stable kebab-case lint id. Merge key when composing with extends parent.

kind.description: Workspace-spanning lint algorithm. AIP-18 per-collection lints (missing-owner, overdue, required-field, etc.) live on COLLECTION.md and are NOT redeclared here. 'custom' delegates to a host-defined check identified by `id`.

severity.description: Lint severity. Children may soften; governance policies MAY forbid softening below `error`.

params.description: Kind-specific parameters. e.g. { days: 14 } for stale-tree; { axis: applicability } for scope-mismatch.

workflow.description: OPTIONAL — default AIP-15 WORKFLOW.md path or ref. Routine workflow run against work items in this workspace.

approvalClass.description: Approval class for mutations. 'auto' = no gate; 'always' = every mutation requires approval; 'on-mutate' = approval on field-level mutations; 'policy:<ref>' = delegate to an AIP-7 policy.

auditMutations.description: Whether mutations are audited. ONE-WAY SWITCH: once true at any ancestor, descendants MUST NOT set false. Refusal: work_audit_downgrade (HARD).

defaults.description: Routine workflow defaults. Composes with AIP-15.

homePage.description: OPTIONAL — id of the item to use as the workspace landing page.

display.description: Display hints for UIs that render the workspace. Runtime-agnostic.

metadata.description: Vendor-specific extensions, namespaced under <vendor>. Deep-merged across the extends chain. MUST NOT change the meaning of any spec field.

allOf.description: A view (any manifest with appliesTo set) MUST extend a parent. A workspace-root manifest has neither field.

collectionEntry.description: One collection declaration. Either an inline AIP-18 collection schema, or a ref (path or ws:// URI) optionally aliased and version-pinned.

inline.description: Full AIP-18 collection.schema/v1 frontmatter, parsed in-place. The host registers the collection directly via AIP-18's defineCollection without loading a separate file.

collectionInline.description: Inline collection declaration. Full AIP-18 schema embedded; hosts MUST validate it against the AIP-18 COLLECTION schema before registration.

ref.description: Either a relative path to a COLLECTION.md (file ref) or a ws://collections/<slug> URI (registry import). The host loads the referenced collection via AIP-18 and registers it under its name (or the alias, if set).

alias.description: OPTIONAL — workspace-local name to expose the collection under. Items in this workspace reference the alias, not the upstream name. Two collections resolving to the same effective name (alias or upstream) is a HARD failure: work_collection_alias_conflict.

version.description: OPTIONAL — semver range (e.g. "1.x", "^1.2", "1.2.0"). When set, schema bumps outside the range fail with collection_item_schema_pinned_drift (HARD) at item load time.

collectionRef.description: Collection ref declaration. Either a file path (./.. /COLLECTION.md) or a registry URI (ws://collections/<slug>).
